content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN elements. Count the number of positive integers XXX satisfying the following condition :


(X‚äēAi)<Ai(X \oplus A_i) < A_i(X‚äēAi‚Äč)<Ai‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N, where ‚äē\oplus‚äē represents the Bitwise XOR operator.
It can be proven that the answer is always finite under the given constraints.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,...,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid positive integers XXX.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test 1 : The only number satisfying the constraint is 111.

1‚äē1=0<11 \oplus 1 = 0 < 11‚äē1=0<1
1‚äē3=2<31 \oplus 3 = 2 < 31‚äē3=2<3

Test Case 2 : It can be proven that there is no valid number.
